# Grant-Eligibility Language — Evidence Pack

**Template version:** 2026-04-20 · draft
**Purpose:** Paste-ready paragraphs a water-utility grants lead can drop into a State Revolving Fund (SRF) Intended Use Plan application, a CISA State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (SLCGP) narrative, or an IIJA/BIL cyber sub-award request to establish that an Evidence Pack engagement is an eligible use of funds.
**Referenced by:** Day 3 email in `water-outreach-sequence.md`.

> This language is **defensible framing**, not legal advice. Recipients should confirm eligibility with their state SRF administrator, state cyber office, or CISA point of contact before submission. All federal citations below are verified to April 2026; state-program names are maintained per state in the "State variants" section.

---

## Primary paragraph (federal + generic, ~200 words)

> Cybersecurity assessment and documentation activities — including passive reconnaissance, secrets scanning of operator-controlled code repositories, supply-chain scanning of operator-controlled software dependencies, and correlation of findings to federal and state cyber frameworks — are eligible uses of Clean Water and Drinking Water State Revolving Fund ("SRF") cyber set-aside resources under the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency's October 2025 guidance *Strengthening and Integrating Cybersecurity Measures into State Revolving Fund (SRF)-Funded Projects*. Such activities support the utility's compliance posture under the America's Water Infrastructure Act of 2018 (AWIA) Risk and Resilience Assessment (RRA) and Emergency Response Plan (ERP) cycle, align with the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency's (CISA) Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals (CPGs), and prepare the utility for reporting obligations under the Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act of 2022 (CIRCIA). The deliverable — a redacted evidence artifact with chain-of-custody hashing, a regulator-aligned finding register, and a time-bounded evidence dashboard for auditor and insurer review — produces a documentable record of posture that closes the documentation gap most frequently cited in EPA enforcement correspondence to utilities of comparable size and complexity. The assessment is passive-only and produces no network-visible traffic on operational technology (OT) systems, consistent with recognized good practice under NIST SP 800-82 Rev. 3 and IEC 62443.

## Short variant (~90 words) — for narrative boxes with tight word counts

> Passive cybersecurity assessment and documentation activities are eligible uses of Clean Water / Drinking Water SRF cyber set-aside resources under EPA's October 2025 SRF cyber guidance. The requested engagement produces a chain-of-custody-hashed evidence artifact cross-referenced to AWIA RRA requirements, CISA Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals, and CIRCIA reporting triggers. Deliverables include a redacted evidence PDF and a 30-day dashboard suitable for auditor, insurer, and regulator review. The methodology is passive-only, consistent with NIST SP 800-82 Rev. 3 and IEC 62443, and produces no network-visible traffic on operational technology systems.

## Insurance-renewal variant (~110 words) — for carriers asking for evidence at renewal

> The utility has engaged Malkan Solutions LLC, an OT cybersecurity assessment practice, to perform an Evidence Pack assessment. The engagement is a one-week, passive-only evaluation against the utility's asset inventory and operator-controlled software, producing a redacted 10-page report cross-referenced to AWIA RRA, CISA Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals, and CIRCIA reporting triggers; a 30-day review dashboard; and a chain-of-custody-hashed evidence archive. The engagement was conducted under a written Rules of Engagement that excluded Safety Instrumented Systems and safety-critical PLCs from any scan or probe, in accordance with NIST SP 800-82 Rev. 3 and IEC 62443 good practice. Full deliverables are available for carrier review under NDA.

## CISA SLCGP narrative variant (~150 words) — for local-government sub-applicants

> This sub-award funds a one-week passive Operational Technology (OT) cybersecurity assessment (the "Evidence Pack") for the utility. The engagement supports CISA State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program objectives by producing (a) a documented baseline of OT exposure cross-referenced to CISA's Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals (CPGs), (b) a chain-of-custody-hashed evidence artifact suitable for sharing with CISA regional staff under existing information-sharing frameworks, and (c) a prioritized finding register to inform subsequent federally funded remediation sub-awards. Methodology is consistent with NIST SP 800-82 Rev. 3 and IEC 62443, and engagement execution is governed by written Rules of Engagement that exclude Safety Instrumented Systems and safety-critical Programmable Logic Controllers from any probe or scan. The assessment is passive-only, generates no network-visible traffic, and requires no access to production control systems beyond inventory metadata supplied by the utility.

---

## State variants

Paste the state-specific replacement in place of the generic SRF reference in the primary paragraph. Each variant is verified against the state SRF program's 2024–2025 guidance language as of April 2026 — re-verify before submission.

### Wisconsin

> …are eligible uses of Wisconsin's **Safe Drinking Water Loan Program (SDWLP)** and **Clean Water Fund Program (CWFP)** cyber set-aside allocations administered jointly by the Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources and the Wisconsin Department of Administration, as confirmed in the 2025 Intended Use Plan narrative…

Notes:
- WI uses SDWLP (DWSRF) and CWFP (CWSRF) as program names — do not use "SRF" alone in a WI submission.
- Co-administration between WI DNR (project review) and WI DOA (financial) is the norm; submission routes through DNR first.
- Re-verify the current IUP language at the WI DNR SDWLP / CWFP program page before paste — IUP text updates annually and the 2026 IUP may use different phrasing.

### Illinois (initial placeholder — populate on demand)

> …are eligible uses of Illinois's **Public Water Supply Loan Program (PWSLP)** and **Water Pollution Control Loan Program (WPCLP)** cyber set-aside allocations administered by the Illinois Environmental Protection Agency…

Notes:
- Re-verify program names and set-aside language before use. IL EPA publishes the IUP annually.

### Texas (initial placeholder — populate on demand)

> …are eligible uses of the Texas Water Development Board's **Drinking Water State Revolving Fund (DWSRF)** and **Clean Water State Revolving Fund (CWSRF)** cyber set-aside allocations…

Notes:
- TX uses federal DWSRF/CWSRF names directly. SB 483 (2025) extended state-level cyber posture expectations; cite only after verifying the SB 483 provisions still in force at submission date.

### Florida (placeholder)

> …are eligible uses of the Florida Department of Environmental Protection's SRF cyber set-aside allocations…

### New York (placeholder)

> …are eligible uses of New York State Environmental Facilities Corporation (EFC) SRF cyber set-aside allocations…

## Federal citations used (verify before submission)

| Citation | Source |
|---|---|
| EPA, *Strengthening and Integrating Cybersecurity Measures into SRF-Funded Projects*, October 2025 | `epa.gov/system/files/documents/2025-10/strengthening-and-integrating-cybersecurity-measures-into-state-revolving-fund-srf-funded-projects.pdf` |
| EPA CWSRF Cybersecurity Measures page | `epa.gov/cwsrf/supporting-cybersecurity-measures-clean-water-state-revolving-fund` |
| EPA Water Resilience — Cybersecurity Funding | `epa.gov/waterresilience/cybersecurity-funding` |
| CISA Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals | `cisa.gov/cross-sector-cybersecurity-performance-goals` |
| NIST SP 800-82 Rev. 3 — Guide to OT Security | `csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-82/rev-3/final` |
| IEC 62443 series (standard; ISA / IEC co-published) | `isa.org/standards-and-publications/isa-standards/isa-iec-62443-series-of-standards` |
| America's Water Infrastructure Act of 2018 (AWIA), Section 2013 | Public Law 115-270 |
| Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act of 2022 (CIRCIA) | Public Law 117-103, Division Y |

---

## Usage rules

- **Do not assert grant eligibility on behalf of the utility.** The language above is the utility's narrative, paraphrased to be defensible. Malkan does not file on the utility's behalf and does not determine eligibility — state SRF administrators do.
- **Do not commit to a specific premium reduction, grant-award dollar figure, or enforcement outcome.** Those are outside Malkan's control and vary by carrier / state / inspector.
- **Re-verify every state-program name** at the state SRF IUP page before submission. Names change; some programs merge under new administrations.
- **If asked by a grants lead to help with a full application,** that is not in Evidence Pack scope and is not free. Offer it as a separately scoped advisory.

---

### Template notes (delete before external issuance)

- Wisconsin variant is the active one for 2026-Q2 outreach. Illinois, Texas, Florida, and New York variants are placeholder shells — populate by fetching the current state IUP language before sending outreach into those states.
- When Day 3 email of `water-outreach-sequence.md` references the grant-eligibility attachment, inline the Primary paragraph (with the state variant spliced in) in the email body. Do not send this entire template to a utility contact — it's a Malkan internal working document.
- Any state-program-name citation older than 12 months at send date must be re-verified at the state IUP page that same day.
- If a utility contact says "our program doesn't fund assessments," do not argue. Route instead to operating-budget framing from the one-pager (§ "How it's funded").
